title: New Beginning
html:
"Everything's all set up," Lucas says, setting his hand flat on the worn wooden table. There's something a little tight in his round black eyes, though his face doesn't show much.

He has curly black hair and skin that's darkened from years of working under the sun most days. In rice country, lighter skin gets read as money. Lucas's doesn't say that. He's 16 years old today and has been doing construction work and fishing since he was old enough to be useful at either.

"Wish my parents were here," he murmurs. Their faces have started to fade, which is something he notices and doesn't like. It's been six years since the fish market accident that killed them both. He was 10 when it happened, left in a village too stretched to absorb another child, and he understood that at the time. The villagers helped where they could, gave him household work and a meal when there was one to spare, and he learned everything else on his own.

The cake sits at the center of the table with a single candle burning. His phone plays the birthday song from the counter behind him.

"Mom, Dad, today your son Lucas turns 16," he says, scanning the room once before he sighs. "Wish you could see this."

He watches the candle for a moment. Birthdays used to feel like something worth waiting for. Now they mostly arrive as a reminder that another year has passed and the room is the same. He bends toward the flame to blow it out, and static crackles in his ear without warning.

"Hmm? What's that?" He pulls back.

Before he can work it into anything sensible, a clean electronic voice fills the room.

---

"HUMAN OF EARTH! INITIATING EARTH MUTATION PROGRAM PHASE 1!"

---

"Huh? Phase one of what?" he mutters. 'Auditory hallucinations.' He lives alone with his nearest neighbor 12 feet away, and there's no other explanation that holds. He decides to ignore it, bends back down to blow out the candle, and cuts himself a slice of cake.

The strawberry grape cake is better than he expected, which he notes with mild surprise. He finishes the rest without thinking too much about it, sets his phone down after the song ends, and stares at the dark room for a moment before making his way to bed.

He's almost asleep when a scream cuts through from outside, distant but sharp. He pulls the blanket over his head. "Don't people know someone might be trying to sleep," he mutters, and the night settles back into silence.

***

January 21, 2029

Sunday is a good day to wake up on. Lucas stretches on the bed, registers that he has no work today, and decides within about thirty seconds that he'll catch something for breakfast rather than deal with whatever's left in the kitchen. He heads outside.

The boat is gone from where he left it. He stands at the bank and scans the full length of the lake, taking his time, and finds nothing. 'Theft seems unlikely.' The boat was old and in bad shape, not worth anyone's effort. He stays with it another few seconds to be certain, then shifts his attention to the rest of the bank.

A fish sits near the shore where the boat used to be, about 30 cm long, in open shallow water. Lucas has been fishing this lake long enough to name most species without having to think about it, and this one doesn't match anything he knows. What's stranger is where it sits. Fish that grow to 30 cm do it by staying away from anything that might eat them, and right now this one is sitting practically at his feet, in the open, with no apparent concern about that.

He retrieves his spear from inside and approaches from the side.

"Swoosh!" The spear catches the fish cleanly and green blood spreads through the water around the wound. He stares at it. 'Is that safe to eat.' The color is unusual, but it's a clean catch on a day without a boat and he has cooked strange fish before. He drags it out, cleans it, and fries it with soy sauce.

The first bite makes him set his chopstick down. "Wow," he says, genuinely surprised. "That's actually really good." He finishes the whole thing at the table and turns the situation over in the back of his mind without landing anywhere useful.

Static crackles in his ear sharper than the night before, and a holographic screen materializes in the air in front of him, fully solid in the morning light. Lucas reads it and then reads it again.

---

[Mutated Fish Species Detected, Commencing System Profile!]

[Opening System!]

---

He has seen terms like stats and levels in the games on his phone. Seeing them in the air in front of the window is a different matter. His first instinct is alien technology, which he sets aside almost immediately once the phrase "mutated fish" surfaces from the announcement the night before. The two things are probably related.

---

Name: Lucas Tharla

Age: 16 years old

Race: Human

Gender: Male

Class: None

Subclass: None

Subclass: None

Subclass: None

Level: 0 (0/10)

Stats:

Agi: 10 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Str: 20 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Int: 25 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Vit: 25 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Unallocated Stats Point: 0

Skills:

Unallocated Skills Point: 0

---

'Not much to go on.' He closes the screen and goes back outside.

About 10 fish matching the one he caught are visible in the shallows near the bank. He watches them from the edge of the water without moving closer, and after a minute two of them begin circling each other in tight turns that get shorter and faster. The fight is quick: one clean bite, and one fish goes still while the other pulls away. What happens next makes Lucas go very still too. The surviving fish grows, right there in the shallows, gaining size from the dead one until it's noticeably bigger than when the fight started.

'That's new.' He's already reaching for the spear when the thought finishes.

"Slash!" The spear enters the water and catches the now-40-cm fish, and Lucas pulls it out intending to get a good look at it. The system screen opens before he can start.

---

[Level 2 Mutated Fish Killed, Awarding 10 Exp!]

[Leveling Exp Reach: 10/10!]

[Leveling Up: Level 1!]

[Receiving 2 Stats Points and 1 Skills Point!]

---

"So I can level up," Lucas says, the same way he'd note that the price of rice went up. He says "System" and the screen opens.

---

[Opening System!]

Name: Lucas Tharla

Age: 16 years old

Race: Human

Gender: Male

Class: None

Subclass: None

Subclass: None

Subclass: None

Level: 1 (0/50)

Stats:

Agi: 10 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Str: 20 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Int: 25 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Vit: 25 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Unallocated Stats Point: 2

Skills:

Unallocated Skills Point: 1

---

"If skill points are real, how come there's nothing listed under skills," he mutters, but the screen doesn't answer that. He closes it and fries the 40 cm fish, because a catch that size is well above his usual hauls and wasting it seems wrong. He eats it down to the bones over the next hour, then sets his chopsticks down on the empty plate.

With no boat and his rice supply running low, he exits through the back of the house, the only side connected to solid ground, and takes the usual path toward the market. He follows the familiar route without paying much attention, until something makes him slow down.

He hasn't passed anyone since he stepped outside. Not on the path, not in any of the yards he's walked past. He turns and checks both directions. The road is quiet in a way that doesn't fit the hour. He has walked this route enough times to know what it usually looks like on a Sunday morning, and it doesn't look like that. "Huh," he says to no one, standing at the edge of the path, not quite sure what he's looking at.
